title: PACCA Change Manifest
description: Schema for harness change manifests in PACCA. Every behavioral change to PACCA's agent harness ships with a manifest entry that names the failure pattern, root cause, predicted fixes, predicted risks, and constraint level. The next evaluation round verifies the predictions against actual case-level outcomes; rejected changes are reverted at file granularity. Methodology adapted from Lin et al., Agentic Harness Engineering (arXiv:2604.25850, 2026).

iteration.description: Iteration number. Iteration 0 is the baseline; each subsequent iteration corresponds to a harness-iter-N git tag.

iteration_tag.description: The git tag this manifest is bound to. Must match the iteration number.

iso_date.description: Date the manifest was committed, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

author.description: Human author of this manifest. Even when changes are drafted with AI assistance, a human author owns the manifest's predictions.

base_model.description: Identifier of the LLM the harness is tuned against (e.g., 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514'). Recorded because the optimal harness is model-specific; a manifest's predictions are only valid against the base model recorded here.

previous_iteration_tag.description: The iteration tag this manifest builds on. Null only for iteration 0.

summary.description: One-paragraph human-readable summary of what this iteration shipped and why.

changes.description: Ordered list of changes shipped in this iteration. Each change is one logical edit at one constraint level.

verdicts.description: Verdicts on changes from the previous iteration's manifest. Populated when this iteration's evaluation round completes. Each verdict references a change ID from the previous manifest.

id.description: Change ID, sequential within the iteration. Matches the git commit prefix (e.g., 'chg-7' corresponds to a commit message beginning 'chg-7:').

type.description: Type of change. 'new' adds a component; 'improvement' refines an existing one; 'rollback' reverts a prior change; 'removal' deletes a component.

description.description: What was changed. One or two sentences.

files.description: Files modified by this change, relative to repo root. The AHE convention is that one logical change touches files within one constraint level only.

items.description: Path of a file modified by this change, relative to repo root. Allowed roots: src/ (any source), harness/, docs/, tests/, .github/ (CI workflows). Also allowed at repo root: pyproject.toml, requirements*.txt, setup.py, setup.cfg, Dockerfile, .gitignore, README.md, CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE, Makefile. If a harness change needs to touch a path not in this list, extend the pattern in its own commit with a manifest entry justifying the addition.

failure_pattern.description: The class of failure this change addresses. Phrased as a pattern, not a single case (e.g., 'Frontline Nurse retroactively auto-approves after Medical Director escalation' rather than 'case-23 was wrong').

root_cause.description: Why the failure occurs. Distinct from the failure pattern: the pattern names the symptom, the root cause names the mechanism.

evidence.description: Specific cases or trajectory log references that demonstrate the failure pattern. Strong manifests cite at least two pieces of evidence per change.

source.description: Case ID, trajectory log path, or audit record reference.

note.description: Optional one-line explanation of what the evidence shows.

predicted_fixes.description: Cases predicted to flip from failing to passing as a result of this change. The AHE paper found self-prediction of fixes is reliable (~5x random precision and recall).

items.description: Case ID from the evaluation suite.

risk_cases.description: Cases predicted to be at risk of regressing as a result of this change. The AHE paper found self-prediction of regressions is barely above random (~2x). Treat this list as a low-confidence claim; the evaluation suite is the safety net.

constraint_level.description: Which component type this change edits. The first seven are AHE/NexAU standard component types; the last four are PACCA-specific harness surfaces (the 7-branch escalation tree, dual-collection ChromaDB, the PROMPT_REGISTRY, and the HIPAA audit log schema).

why_this_component.description: Why this constraint level was chosen over alternatives. Required because the AHE paper's findings hinge on choosing the right level: system prompt edits in isolation tend to regress, while tools/middleware/memory edits tend to compound.

phi_impact.description: PACCA-specific. Whether this change affects how Protected Health Information flows through the system. 'none' = no PHI handling change; 'indirect' = changes logging or audit of PHI-handling code paths; 'direct' = changes what PHI fields are accessed, stored, or transmitted.

audit_relevant.description: PACCA-specific. Whether this change touches code paths required for HIPAA 45 CFR 164.312(b) audit controls or FDA SaMD change-control compliance. If true, the change must be reflected in the immutable PolicyChangeLogEntry per Section 14 of the PRD.

rollback_plan.description: How to revert this change if the next-iteration verdict rejects it. For most file-level changes this is 'git revert <sha>'. For changes that require database migrations or RAG collection updates, document the reverse migration here.

change_id.description: The change ID this verdict applies to.

previous_iteration_tag.description: The iteration tag where this change originally landed.

outcome.description: Verdict, mirroring the AHE paper's vocabulary. 'keep' = working, leave as-is; 'improve' = directionally correct, refine; 'rollback' = not working at this constraint level, revert and re-approach from a different level.

verified_fixes.description: Predicted fixes that materialized — true positives.

missed_fixes.description: Cases that flipped to passing but were not predicted — true positives the manifest didn't claim credit for.

false_predicted_fixes.description: Predicted fixes that did NOT materialize — false positives. A high count here weakens the manifest's authorial credibility for future iterations.

verified_risks.description: Predicted risks that materialized — the change did regress these cases.

unforeseen_regressions.description: Cases that regressed without being predicted as risks. The AHE paper's regression-blindness finding predicts this list will typically be longer than verified_risks.

regression_precision.description: verified_risks / (verified_risks + risks predicted but not realized). Expected to be low per the AHE paper's findings; tracked anyway as a calibration signal.

regression_recall.description: verified_risks / (verified_risks + unforeseen_regressions). Low values flag that the evaluation suite needs more case coverage in the affected area.

notes.description: Optional human commentary on the verdict, especially when outcome is 'rollback' — capturing why the constraint level was wrong is the most valuable text in the manifest for future iterations.
